stimulus: A study led by chemist Adaeze Okonkwo at the University of Cape Town found that ostriches that forage near agricultural runoff retain elevated concentrations of a rare nitrogen isotope, ______blank traceable to the synthetic fertilizers used on nearby fields. Birds with these elevated levels were also found to lay eggs with notably thicker shells on average.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is the punctuation of a supplementary element within a sentence. The comma after “(15N)” pairs with the comma after “isotope” to separate the supplementary element “nitrogen-15 (15N)” from the rest of the sentence. The supplementary element defines the “rare nitrogen isotope.”

Choice A is incorrect because it fails to set off the supplementary element with a closing comma. Choice B is incorrect because it leaves the supplementary element unclosed and inserts an unwarranted comma before the parentheses. Choice C is incorrect because no comma is needed before the parentheses, which already separate “15N” from the rest of the sentence.
